title: Do COVID-19 cases follow a similar transition path? Evidence from Indian states
author: Akram, Rath, et al.

steps.title: Methodology
steps.instruction: To identify the transition paths of COVID-19, this study implements a novel approach propounded by Phillips and Sul [29]. This test is based on the clustering algorithm that clusters the club of countries or states based on transition paths. Moreover, this method considers the nonlinear time-varying factor unlike the Solow-Swan growth model [30,31]. The most important feature of this test is that it does not count the ‚Äúcointegration or unit root properties‚ÄĚ and it considers the transitional dynamics in addition to long-run behaviour of the variable. In other words, it amends the bias that ascends from both ‚Äústationary and non‚Äźstationary or a mix of both series in the panel due to misspecification‚ÄĚ. All conventional unit root tests and simple regression may not account the heterogeneity of cross-sections as a result, they may provide misleading results [32]. Thus, PS include both ‚Äúcommon and cross-section‚ÄĚ specific behaviour and find multiple steady-states (club convergence) unlike ‚Äúbeta and sigma convergence‚ÄĚ, which is built on a steady-state.

steps.title: Methodology
steps.instruction: PS test is quite familiar in the recent empirical studies of convergence. This study uses the PS test to examine convergence and transition paths of COVID-19 cases by using subnational data of India. We begin by writing the single factor model as follows:(1)COVID‚ąí19it=őīiőľt+uitWhere COVID‚ąí19it considered as confirmed and recovered cases of COVID-19 in the case of Indian states (i) at time (t). In Eq.¬†(1), őīi refers to the ‚Äúidiosyncratic distance‚ÄĚ between the common factor őľtand systematic part of COVID‚ąí19it. őľt stands for common behaviour of COVID‚ąí19it and uit represents error term. Further, Eq.¬†(1) is decomposed into Ait which refers to the ‚Äúsystematic‚ÄĚ components and Bit refers to ‚Äútransitory‚ÄĚ components.(2)COVID‚ąí19it=Ait+Bit

Furthermore, coefficients of őīitshow the common factor share of (őľt) each cross-section in a group of cross-sections. The convergence approach based on PS follows a dynamic-process and őīitadvises ‚Äútransition paths‚ÄĚ. The model cannot be fitted without imposing few restrictions on őīit and ut. This arises in the long run when cross-section variance is zero (hit‚Üí0). Thus, PS test suggests the following assumption for convergence (club) for the ‚Äúsemiparametric form of time-varying coefficients őīit‚ÄĚ:(5)őīit=őīi+ŌÉitőĺit
